cases.title: Duplicate registration attempt blocked per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with available seats and active registration window
Participant already has an active registration (Registered) for the event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) for the same participant a second time
Query active registration rows for the participant/event pair
cases.expected: Registration is rejected with ApiError code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE and HTTP status 409; no second active row is created; testing-plan §3 row 'Duplicate registration attempt → blocked' is satisfied

cases.title: Second registration attempt persists no duplicate row in database transaction
preconditions: Event is in RegistrationOpen state with available seats
Registration window is active
Participant already has an active registration (Registered) for the event
Database transaction boundaries enabled for registration module
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) for the same participant a second time
Query registrations table for (eventId, participantId) where state is active after rollback or rejection
cases.expected: Registration is rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE (409); exactly one active registration row remains; existing registration state unchanged at DB boundary

cases.title: POST registrations duplicate returns HTTP 409 with typed error envelope
preconditions: Published event with registration opened and available capacity
Participant JWT used to successfully POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations once
Fresh Idempotency-Key header for the duplicate attempt
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with the same participant Authorization bearer token a second time
Parse error response body and GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with error.code REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE, user-safe message per API error contract, requestId, and timestamp; GET registration-status still returns the original registration unchanged

cases.title: Participant UI blocks duplicate registration with clear feedback
preconditions: Participant logged in and already Registered for an open event
Event detail page shows current Registered status badge or label
steps: Navigate to event detail for the event where participant is already registered
Attempt to register again via UI (register button, retry, or equivalent action if exposed)
Observe inline or toast error messaging and registration status area
cases.expected: UI does not create a second registration; participant sees a clear duplicate-registration message (inline error, disabled CTA, or toast) consistent with server REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE semantics

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when participant is already Waitlisted
preconditions: Event is full with waitlistEnabled true
Participant already has active Waitlisted registration for the event
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for the same participant and event again
cases.expected: Rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE (409); waitlistPosition and single Waitlisted row unchanged; no second waitlist entry created; at-most-once applies to all active states not only Registered

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when participant is already CheckedIn
preconditions: Participant has active registration in CheckedIn state for the event
Event registration window still open (if applicable)
steps: Attempt registrationService.register for the same participant and event
cases.expected: Rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; CheckedIn registration remains the sole active record

cases.title: Re-registration allowed after valid cancellation releases inactive state
preconditions: Participant previously Registered, then cancelled registration (CancelledByUser) before deadline per BR-07
Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
No other active registration for participant on this event
steps: Invoke registrationService.register for the same participant after cancellation completed
Query active registration for participant/event pair
cases.expected: beforeState CancelledByUser (inactive) permits new registration; afterState Registered on a new or reactivated row; BR-01 satisfied (at most one active registration at a time); not treated as duplicate

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate registration requests from same participant yield single active row
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Same participantId used in parallel registrationService.register calls with no prior registration
steps: Fire two or more concurrent register calls for the same participantId and eventId
Count active registration rows for the participant/event pair
cases.expected: Exactly one request succeeds creating an active registration; all other concurrent attempts are rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE or equivalent conflict; database never holds more than one active registration per participant/event

cases.title: Duplicate POST with new Idempotency-Key still rejected (dedupe is not idempotency)
preconditions: Participant successfully registered via POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Idempotency-Key K1
Second POST prepared with different Idempotency-Key K2
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Idempotency-Key K2 for the same authenticated participant
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; deduplication guard applies independently of idempotency key reuse

cases.title: Dedupe validation runs before capacity assignment in registration pipeline
preconditions: Event full with waitlist disabled (would otherwise reject with REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL)
Participant already has active Registered registration
steps: Attempt second registration for same participant via registrationService.register
Inspect error code on failure
cases.expected: Failure code is REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE (not capacity/full rejection), confirming pipeline step 3 (dedup active registration) executes before step 4 (capacity decision) per validation pipeline

cases.title: P0 invariant: at most one active registration per participant per event in persistence
preconditions: Database with registration module constraints enabled
Any sequence of registration attempts including retries and concurrent calls for same participant/event
steps: After exercising duplicate-block scenarios, query registrations for participantId and eventId where state is active (Requested, Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn)
cases.expected: Row count for active states is never greater than 1; aligns with testing plan P0 severity for duplicate active registration defects

cases.title: Flow B duplicate registration step is blocked after waitlist entry
preconditions: E2E Flow B fixture: participant already Waitlisted on a full event
Valid participant JWT and fresh Idempotency-Key
steps: Execute Flow B AC-03 step: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations again for the waitlisted participant
Verify error response before proceeding with cancellation/promotion steps
cases.expected: HTTP 409 with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; original Waitlisted registration unchanged with same registrationId and waitlistPosition

cases.title: OrganizerAdmin and OrganizerStaff denied POST registrations (cannot trigger duplicate path)
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Valid JWT for OrganizerAdmin (or OrganizerStaff scoped to the event)
Actor has no participant registration for the event
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with OrganizerAdmin bearer token and Idempotency-Key
Repeat POST with OrganizerStaff bearer token for the same event
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 unauthorized; no registration row is created; Register for event is Participant-only per roles-permissions matrix — duplicate prevention is not reachable by non-participant roles

cases.title: Duplicate blocked when registration is still in Requested state
preconditions: Participant has registration row in Requested state (in-flight, not yet accepted or rejected)
Partial unique index on (event_id, participant_id) covers Requested among active states
steps: Attempt a second registrationService.register for the same participant and event before first request completes or transitions out of Requested
cases.expected: Second attempt rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE; Requested row remains the only active registration; aligns with database active-state constraint (Requested, Registered, Waitlisted, CheckedIn)
